title: Buttery Shattering Australian Sirloin Steak Wrap
description: A monsoon-inspired Australian dish featuring a buttery, shattering sirloin steak served in a long-fermentation dough ti-leaf wrap, suitable for Ayurvedic sattvic diets.

ingredients.name: ti leaf wraps
ingredients.notes: long-fermentation dough, made from native Australian flours and wild yeast.

instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat (180°C).
Season the sirloin steak with salt, pepper, and chopped lemongrass.
Marinate the steak in a mixture of coconut oil, galangal, and fish sauce for at least 30 minutes.
Remove the steak from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Grill the steak for 5 minutes per side, or until it reaches your desired level of doneness.
Let the steak rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly against the grain.
Brush the ti-leaf wraps with coconut oil and warm them on the grill for 1 minute on each side.
Assemble the wraps by placing a portion of the sliced steak in the center, followed by your choice of garnishes.
Serve the wraps immediately, with melted butter on top.
